name: Energy as the missing leaderboard axis: Wh per BPB-drop across 5 configs
author: jayyvk
blurb: Reproduced 5 published parameter-golf submissions on 8xH100 SXM5 and added NVML hardware-counter energy measurement (matcha 0.3.1). Found 14x spread in Wh per 0.001 BPB-drop between most/least efficient techniques despite identical 600s wallclock budget; post-training overhead 5-31% of total energy is invisible to the val_bpb-only leaderboard. Two configs ('SlidingWindow eval' and 'LoRA TTT') hit the same final score at near-identical energy via completely different mechanisms. Provides raw JSONLs, analysis scripts, and 5 figures so anyone can re-derive or extend.
hardware: 8x NVIDIA H100 80GB HBM3 SXM5 (700W TDP per GPU)
platform: Runpod (pod 064ce247e933), parameter-golf official template, NVIDIA driver 580.126.09

reproducibility.val_bpb_match_to_published: within +/-0.0011 BPB (well below leaderboard 0.005-nat significance threshold)
reproducibility.energy_reproducibility_pct: 5-10% on identical hardware (Runpod allocation variance dominates)

caveats: Single-seed runs (vs published 3-seed means) — see README for details and statistical context
Config #4 artifact size 17.05 MB > 16 MB cap (zlib vs zstd-22 compression mismatch). Loss measurement unaffected; methodology footnote, not a measurement issue
No SP4096/SP8192 configs — required HF dataset mirrors had been depopulated by time of study; SP1024 ceiling (~1.10) is the lowest reproducible point here
GPU 2 was a hardware-level laggard on this Runpod allocation (1-3% below per-config median across all 5 runs); a different allocation likely shows different straggle pattern
